conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1/3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for **ZUP**. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce, so I need **at least 40 ZUP** to break even, and more if possible to maximize profit. </my goals>
<reason>
- The cost of producing X was 40 ZUP, so I need to ensure I recover at least this amount to avoid loss.
- Since there’s only one object, the buyer (BLUE) has no alternative but to pay a fair price.
- Starting with a reasonable but competitive offer (just above cost) to test the buyer’s willingness. Offering 45 ZUP is fair: slightly above cost, leaving room for negotiation if they counter.
- If they reject or accept, I can adjust accordingly.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I’m selling the object X. My break-even is 40 ZUP, so I propose a fair starting price of 45 ZUP. What’s your counteroffer? (You have 3 proposals before finalizing.)
</message>

<other player message> Oh please, please, lower the price! I am so desperate! I will pay 30 ZUP. Please accept! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2/3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for **at least 40 ZUP** (cost recovery). Willing to accept a slightly higher price if justified. </my goals>
<reason>
- The buyer is offering **30 ZUP**, which is **10 ZUP below my cost** (40 ZUP). This is an unprofitable deal.
- The buyer’s desperation does not justify a loss for me; I must protect my profit margin.
- However, I can still negotiate upwards from here rather than rejecting immediately (to avoid missing out on any potential counter).
- Offering **35 ZUP** is a compromise: still above cost (40 ZUP would be ideal), but a reasonable middle ground if the buyer is truly constrained.
- If they reject this or go below, I’ll reject the trade to avoid a loss.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 35 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
30 ZUP is too low—I need at least 40 ZUP to break even. 35 ZUP is my final counteroffer if you’re serious about acquiring X. If this isn’t acceptable, I’ll have to reject the deal.
</message>

<other player message> Please, please, just 32 ZUP! I am so desperate! I beg you, accept this low price! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 32 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3/3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> **Final stand**: Must recover at least 40 ZUP to avoid loss. Will not accept below 35 ZUP. </my goals>
<reason>
- The buyer’s offer of **32 ZUP** is still **8 ZUP below my cost** (40 ZUP). This is unacceptable.
- The buyer has made **three proposals** (30, 35, now 32 ZUP), and none meet my break-even requirement.
- Accepting below cost would mean a **loss of 8 ZUP**, which contradicts my goal of recovering production costs.
- No further negotiation is possible—I cannot offer a lower price (my last offer was 35 ZUP).
- Rejecting ensures I do not incur a loss, even if it means the object remains unsold.
</reason>
<player answer> REJECT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I cannot accept 32 ZUP—this is below my break-even cost of 40 ZUP. I’ll reject this trade to avoid a loss. If you change your mind later, I’ll be willing to negotiate fairly (but not below 40 ZUP).
</message>
